resume.raw_text:
=== Bianka Pena - Resume.pdf ===
BIANKA NICOLLE PENA MARCELINO

Temple, PA 19560

6469721673 - bianka.pena.marcelino@gmail.com

WEBSITES, PORTFOLIOS, PROFILES

•

linkedin.com/in/bianka-pena-708173232

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Dedicated Psychology and Biology student with a passion for understanding the complexities

of  human behavior, health, and the factors that influence lives. Extensive experience in HIV

research, clinical care, and public health has fostered a strong commitment to making a

meaningful impact in underserved communities. Eager to further develop research skills and

contribute to advancements in epidemiology, focusing on addressing health disparities and

promoting wellness. Proactive learner driven by a desire to translate academic knowledge into

practical solutions that enhance community health outcomes.

SKILLS

•

•

•

•

Client advocacy

Relationship building

Strategic planning

Time management

•

•

•

•

Teamwork

Conflict resolution

Report writing

Analytical thinking

RESEARCH & CLINICAL EXPERIENCE
05/2025 to 05/2026
Counselor Aide

Helio Health – Syracuse, NY

•
•

•
•
•

Provided direct resident care in substance use disorder treatment settings.

Administered medications as a med-certified aide and maintained HIPAA-compliant

documentation.

Monitored resident behavior and medication effects, reporting concerns to clinical staff.

Supported daily routines, crisis de-escalation, and recovery goals across varied shifts.

Here are six original resume statements that reflect high-value responsibilities for the

Counselor's Aide role at Helio Health, filling gaps in the existing work history while

maintaining the user's writing style:

09/2024 to 05/2026

Peer Academic Leader (PAL)

Le Moyne College – Syracuse, NY

•
•
•
•
•

•

Facilitated structured study halls supporting undergraduate academic success.

Coached students on time management, study strategies, and accountability.

Monitored attendance and engagement while maintaining supportive peer relationships.

Mentored students and connected them with campus academic resources.

Developed and implemented engaging academic workshops to enhance student study

skills.

Coordinated with faculty to align tutoring sessions with course objectives and

requirements.

 06/2024 to 05/2026

Research Student

Le Moyne College – Syracuse, NY

•
•
•
•
•

•

Conducted HIV-1 research on pathogenesis and host restriction factors.

Performed cell culture (THP-1), DNA extraction, SDS-PAGE, and Western blotting.

Trained and mentored new lab members in research techniques and lab protocols.

Assisted with experimental design, data analysis, and research presentations.

Assisted in laboratory maintenance, ensuring equipment and supplies were properly

organized.

Collaborated with team members to refine research protocols and improve efficiency.

06/2023 to 08/2023

Summer Research Intern

UASD – Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic

•

•
•
•
•
•

Contributed to a public health research project on maternal mortality and healthcare

access.

Assisted with data collection, organization, and review of  maternal health indicators.

Analyzed social, economic, and healthcare system factors affecting maternal outcomes.

Supported research discussions and summarized key findings.

Assisted in the preparation of  research proposals and grant applications for funding.

Conducted literature reviews to identify relevant studies and inform research

methodologies.

Bachelor of  Science: Psychology

Le Moyne - Syracuse, NY

Bachelor of  Arts: Biology

Le Moyne - Syracuse, NY

EDUCATION

05/2026 

05/2026 

CERTIFICATIONS

•
•

Improving Global Health: Focusing on Quality and Safety

CPR / AED

COMMUNITY SERVICE & VOLUNTEER WORK

•

•

•
•

Wescott Community Center, Syracuse, NY, 2023-09, 2025-08, Supported Older Adult

programs through recreational activities and social engagement., Assisted with food

pantry operations, meal distribution, and one-on-one administrative support for

community members.

Ambassador Volunteer, New York, NY, 2019-10, 2021-06, Designed and led a community

food bank initiative addressing food insecurity., Participated in civic engagement, Census

outreach, and collaborative community-based mentorship projects.

Active member of  the Psychology, Spanish, and Chemistry Club

Selected as an Ignite Fellowship recipient for three consecutive summers, 2023-2025.

CAMPUS ENGAGEMENT

LANGUAGES

Spanish

Full Professional

English

Full Professional
